rule_text: PostgreSQL psql encoding rules: never use accents, ennes, or special characters in comments or string patterns inside psql -c queries. Always verify columns via information_schema.columns before querying. core_brain is a schema not a table.
context: MCUM health check via psql commands: encoding errors 0xfa/0xf1/0xed, wrong column names, parallel call cancellations
conclusion: Use ASCII-only SQL, sequential psql execution, pg_isready before query, always schema.table format
fix: pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 first, then psql with fully qualified names
errors_observed: secuencia de bytes no valida para codificacion UTF8: 0xfa
secuencia de bytes no valida para codificacion UTF8: 0xf1 0x6f 0x2f 0x64
secuencia de bytes no valida para codificacion UTF8: 0xed 0x63 0x75
prevention_rules: No accents in psql comments or string patterns
